title: About Yvette - Yvette Cooper MP - Yvette Cooper MP
meta_description: Labour MP for Pontefract, Castleford, Knottingley & Altofts and Foreign Secretary
text_excerpt: About Yvette - Yvette Cooper MP - Yvette Cooper MP Skip to main content Yvette Cooper MP Labour MP for Pontefract, Castleford, Knottingley & Altofts and Foreign Secretary Join Get Involved Donate About Yvette Contact Latest News Surveys Your views More Menu About Yvette Yvette Cooper MP Yvette Cooper has served as the Member of Parliament for Pontefract, Castleford and Knottingley since 1997, working closely with local communities across the Five Towns to represent coalfield communities and engage on issues such as jobs, public services, and public safety. Much of her constituency work has centred on engaging with residents, holding surgeries, visiting local groups, and supporting thousands of people through casework across a wide range of issues. If you live in the Pontefract, Castleford and Knottingley constituency, you can contact Yvette here . Alongside her constituency responsibilities, Yvette has held a number of Government roles. She has represented Britain on the world stage as Foreign Secretary, promoting British industries and working for peace and fairness globally. She also served as Home Secretary, where she led the fight to restore neighbourhood policing after 14 years of Conservative cuts. During the last Labour Government, Yvette held a range of ministerial roles, including responsibility for rolling out Sure Start children’s centres, supporting families across Britain.
